mnemonics: NEW is the Key: Think of the 'new' keyword as the key that unlocks and allocates a new house (object) for your blueprint (class).
keyPoints: Declaration (ClassName objectName;) only creates a reference variable that can point to an object, but does not create the object itself.
Instantiation (using the 'new' keyword) is required to actually allocate memory and create the object.
The 'new' keyword calls the class constructor to initialize the new object.
commonMistakes: Trying to use an object reference that has only been declared but not instantiated (e.g., MyClass obj; obj.someMethod();), which causes a NullPointerException.
Confusing object declaration with object creation, thinking the statement 'MyClass obj;' is enough to have a usable object.
